rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees involving ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE annexes; avoid introducing new package-qualified names.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid AADL scopes based on whether they reference component ports or implementation details.
Do not introduce assumptions or guarantees that reference non-visible or non-existent AADL features or types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only features and types present in the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or vice versa.
Do not assume that AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL scope and component type vs implementation distinctions
Defensive rules for AGREE type mismatches and invalid references
